# Cover Letter — Clark Nuber Solutions Architect

---

Dear Clark Nuber Hiring Team,

There are not many accounting firms at Clark Nuber's scale that have explicitly committed to staying independent. In a profession where the audit opinion is one of the few mechanisms of accountability that businesses and investors actually rely on, that commitment matters. A firm oriented around a private equity exit is a firm with a conflict between the work and the return — and that conflict doesn't stay contained. I'm applying to Clark Nuber specifically because the mission here hasn't been subordinated to something else.

I started at RANDY's Worldwide in March with no onboarding. I was given an account in Great Plains, shown how to export data and apply transactions, and within a week I was writing Visual Basic for Applications macros to pull mass invoice data and dissect it into accounts. That pattern — handed a new system, productive within days — has defined how I engage with unfamiliar tooling. When large client invoice payoffs introduced reconciliation failures, I built audit workflows to split, match, and flag underpays, overpays, and duplicates against pulled GP data. Each time something broke I investigated, fixed the root cause, and the system became more robust for it. That's the Tier 2 support loop: not just resolving the ticket, but closing the gap that produced it.

The repeatable process work I'm most proud of came from a different angle. At UW Earth & Space Sciences, staff were filling out confusing Excel templates to initiate purchase and travel requests — templates that broke depending on which version of Excel you had. I replaced the workflow with a Google Forms survey backed by a Python script that reformatted the submitted data into the required Excel structure and routed it automatically to the right person. IT adopted it and ran it until they could build a permanent replacement. That's the handoff that matters: a solution repeatable enough that it outlasts the person who built it.

I want to address CaseWare and Datablend directly. My background is building these operations from scratch — file system navigation and structured document management tooling, ETL pipelines in VBA and Python, middleware that pulls from one system, transforms, and delivers to another. I haven't worked with CaseWare or Datablend specifically, but the conceptual glue is there.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,
Simon-Hans Edasi
simonhansedasi@gmail.com
